articles.title: How I Built a 10x Faster Rust JSON Parser
articles.why: Practical SIMD techniques you can apply today
articles.description: A deep dive into SIMD optimizations and zero-copy parsing techniques that pushed throughput beyond existing solutions.

articles.title: The Hidden Costs of Microservices at Scale
articles.why: Know the tradeoffs before you commit to the architecture
articles.description: After five years running microservices in production, here's what nobody told us about operational overhead.

articles.title: Why I Switched Back from Neovim to VS Code
articles.why: A grounded take on the editor debate worth bookmarking
articles.description: Productivity isn't about the tool — it's about the workflow. A pragmatic developer reflects on the editor wars.

articles.title: SQLite is Not a Toy Database
articles.why: You're probably reaching for Postgres when you don't need to
articles.description: Most developers severely underestimate what SQLite can handle. Benchmarks and real-world case studies inside.

articles.title: Demystifying the Linux TCP Stack
articles.why: Rare annotated walkthrough of kernel networking code
articles.description: A kernel contributor explains the path a TCP packet takes from NIC to userspace, with annotated source references.

articles.title: Type Systems Are Not About Preventing Bugs
articles.why: Reframes how you think about types — genuinely useful
articles.description: A thought-provoking essay arguing that the real value of static types is documentation and communication, not correctness.

repos.name: microsoft/TypeScript
repos.why: If you're writing JS without types, this is why the community moved on
repos.description: TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript that compiles to clean JavaScript output.

repos.name: shadcn-ui/ui
repos.why: Copy-paste component library that doesn't lock you into a design system
repos.description: Beautifully designed components built with Radix UI and Tailwind CSS.

repos.name: astral-sh/uv
repos.why: Replaces pip, pip-tools, and virtualenv — worth switching today
repos.description: An extremely fast Python package installer and resolver, written in Rust.

repos.name: ggerganov/llama.cpp
repos.why: Run LLMs locally without a GPU or API key — essential for offline AI work
repos.description: LLM inference in C/C++ — run large language models locally with minimal setup.

repos.name: pocketbase/pocketbase
repos.why: Single binary backend with auth, DB, and realtime — great for solo projects
repos.description: Open Source backend for your next SaaS and Mobile app in one file.

videos.title: The Future of Web Development in 2026
videos.why: Panel of top engineers — rare unfiltered look at where the web is headed
videos.description: Top engineers from Google, Vercel, and Cloudflare debate what the web platform will look like in the next five years. Edge computing, AI-native frameworks, and the death of the SPA era are all on the table.

videos.title: Bun 2.0 is Here — Everything You Need to Know
videos.why: Big release — catch up in 20 minutes instead of reading the changelog
videos.description: Oven drops a massive release with native Windows support, improved bundler performance, and a revamped test runner.

videos.title: Svelte 6 Deep Dive: Runes in Practice
videos.why: Straight from the team — best way to understand the new reactivity model
videos.description: Rich Harris and the Svelte team walk through every breaking change and why the rune-based reactivity model is a game changer.

videos.title: Stop Using Docker for Local Dev
videos.why: Nix flakes are worth understanding — this is the best intro
videos.description: Nix flakes and devenv.sh have made Docker-compose setups obsolete for most local development workflows.

videos.title: Postgres Just Beat Redis at Its Own Game
videos.why: Could simplify your stack significantly if you're already on Postgres
videos.description: With the new RESP protocol extension, Postgres can now serve as a drop-in Redis replacement at comparable speeds.

podcasts.title: Soft Skills Engineering — Ep. 412: Navigating a Toxic Codebase
podcasts.why: Practical advice for a situation most engineers will face eventually
podcasts.description: Dave and Jamison tackle listener questions about inheriting unmaintainable code and how to make progress without burning out.

podcasts.title: Soft Skills Engineering — Ep. 411: How to Ask for a Raise
podcasts.why: Concrete scripts and timing tips you can use in your next 1:1
podcasts.description: The hosts break down how to frame the conversation, when to bring data, and what to do if the answer is no.

kelvinsPick.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications — Free Chapter on Replication
kelvinsPick.description: Martin Kleppmann has released the replication chapter of DDIA as a free read. If you've ever been confused about leader election, quorums, or eventual consistency, this is the clearest explanation you'll find anywhere. Mandatory reading.
